predictions.text: By 2026-10-31, Charles Schwab will launch direct spot trading of both bitcoin (BTC) and ether (ETH) for U.S. retail brokerage customers (not via ETFs), publicly documented on Schwab.com and/or in a Schwab press release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Schwab publishes a product page/help article or press release stating Schwab customers can buy/sell spot BTC and spot ETH directly in Schwab brokerage accounts (or equivalent), and the feature is available to U.S. retail customers. NO if only ETFs/ETPs are offered or if only one of BTC/ETH is supported by the deadline.
monitoring_signals: Schwab press releases and product updates
Schwab crypto-related FAQs/help-center pages
App release notes mentioning crypto spot trading
SEC/FINRA filings or disclosures about crypto custody/partners
predictions.rationale: Schwab has publicly telegraphed intent; the main uncertainty is regulatory/custody execution and timing versus prior crypto cycles.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, the U.S. Department of Justice will file a remedies brief/motion in the Live Nation/Ticketmaster antitrust case that explicitly requests a divestiture of Ticketmaster (or an equivalent structural separation), as shown in a court docket filing or DOJ press release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a DOJ filing (or DOJ press release) in the Live Nation/Ticketmaster matter explicitly seeks divestiture/spin-off/separation of Ticketmaster (or equivalent structural remedy). Resolves NO if no such explicit request appears by the deadline.
monitoring_signals: PACER/courtlistener docket entries for U.S. v. Live Nation/Ticketmaster
DOJ Antitrust Division press releases
Scheduling orders for remedies phase/trial
predictions.rationale: A liability finding increases remedy leverage, but DOJ may still prefer behavioral relief or a slower remedies schedule, making an explicit divestiture request by August uncertain.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, the European Commission will publicly propose an emergency amendment, derogation, or temporary flexibility to ReFuelEU Aviation (or a comparable EU SAF/aviation-fuel rule) explicitly citing jet-fuel supply constraints or disruption risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the European Commission publishes a formal proposal/communication (COM document, press release, or Official Journal-linked proposal) that adds a temporary flexibility/derogation to EU aviation fuel blending/supply requirements and explicitly cites jet-fuel shortage/supply constraints/disruption risk as rationale.
monitoring_signals: European Commission transport/energy press releases
EUR-Lex proposals referencing ReFuelEU Aviation
Airlines/airports lobbying statements about SAF/jet fuel availability
predictions.rationale: A severe jet-fuel squeeze can trigger temporary regulatory flexibility, but EU climate-policy stickiness makes this a low-to-moderate probability outcome.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, at least one of Lufthansa, IAG, Air France-KLM, or Ryanair will issue an earnings-related profit warning or guidance cut that explicitly cites jet-fuel supply constraints and/or jet-fuel price spikes as a material driver.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a company press release, investor presentation, or earnings statement from one of the named airlines includes an explicit guidance cut/profit warning and explicitly mentions jet-fuel supply constraints, jet-fuel shortage, or jet-fuel price spikes as a material cause. NO otherwise.
monitoring_signals: Airline RNS/press releases and investor relations updates
Quarterly earnings calls/transcripts (jet fuel commentary)
IATA/A4E statements on fuel supply
Northwest Europe jet fuel crack spreads and airport NOTAMs about fuel availability
predictions.rationale: If the IEA-style shortage scenario materializes, airlines are likely to reflect it quickly via guidance, but carriers may hedge and frame impacts as price rather than physical constraints.

predictions.text: By 2026-05-31, the U.S. and Iran (directly or via an identified mediator such as Oman or Qatar) will publicly confirm the start of formal talks aimed at de-escalation/ceasefire, via a U.S. State Department/White House statement or Iran MFA statement.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official U.S. (State/White House) or Iranian MFA statement explicitly confirms formal talks have started (not merely 'open channels') and frames them as aimed at de-escalation/ceasefire. NO if only informal/anonymous reports appear or if no official confirmation by deadline.
monitoring_signals: State Department briefings and readouts
Iran MFA statements and IRNA reporting citing official remarks
Oman/Qatar foreign ministry announcements
UN/Swiss channel diplomatic readouts
predictions.rationale: Public signals suggest an off-ramp is being sought; official confirmation depends on battlefield dynamics and domestic politics on both sides.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Netflix will appoint and disclose a new Board Chair (or Executive Chair) distinct from the CEO in a formal filing (8-K, proxy statement) or press release, following Reed Hastings’ exit from the chair/board role.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Netflix publicly discloses (8-K, DEF 14A, annual report, or press release) the appointment of a named Board Chair/Executive Chair who is not the CEO. NO if Netflix keeps no chair role filled/disclosed by deadline or merges it into the CEO role.
monitoring_signals: Netflix SEC filings (8-K, DEF 14A)
Netflix investor relations governance updates
Board committee/lead independent director disclosures
predictions.rationale: Governance transitions often lead to a formalized chair/leadership structure within a year, but Netflix could rely on a lead independent director model instead.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, there will be at least 4 U.S.-listed IPOs in the GICS Industrials sector (or clearly industrial/manufacturing businesses) that each raise at least $500 million in gross proceeds (base deal, excluding overallotment), indicating a sustained reopening of the industrial IPO window.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if IPO databases (e.g., SEC prospectuses plus deal terms) show ≥4 NYSE/Nasdaq IPOs in 2026 that are classified as Industrials (or plainly industrial/manufacturing) and each has base gross proceeds ≥$500M. NO otherwise.
monitoring_signals: IPO calendars and pricing announcements
S-1/F-1 filings for industrial issuers
Industrial IPO ETF/sector performance and volatility regime
Underwriter commentary on reopening issuance
predictions.rationale: A single blockbuster IPO can precede a broader reopening, but sustained industrial issuance depends on equity risk appetite and rates/volatility staying supportive.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the Federal Reserve will publish at least 15 enforcement-action press releases in 2026 involving banks or bank holding companies with total assets under $10 billion (at the time of action or as stated in the release).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if counting 2026 Federal Reserve enforcement press releases yields ≥15 actions where the subject institution’s total assets are < $10B (using assets disclosed in the release or the institution’s most recent call report around the action date). NO otherwise.
monitoring_signals: Federal Reserve enforcement press release feed
Call report assets for cited institutions
Trends in MRA/MRIA and governance-related supervisory messaging
predictions.rationale: Recent actions highlight continued supervisory intensity for smaller institutions, but the annual count is sensitive to enforcement cadence and bundling of actions.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-15, the Federal Reserve’s G.17 release will show U.S. capacity utilization (total industry) at or above 79.5% in at least one monthly report (either the headline series or a revision).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any G.17 Industrial Production and Capacity Utilization release published by 2026-08-15 reports total industry capacity utilization ≥79.5% (seasonally adjusted) for any month shown in the tables (including revised prior months).
monitoring_signals: Monthly G.17 releases and revisions
ISM manufacturing and regional Fed surveys
Industrial production subcomponents tied to energy/defense demand
Supply disruptions that constrain capacity while output holds up
predictions.rationale: Geopolitical supply stress can tighten utilization, but slowing growth could offset, making a near-term ≥79.5% print plausible but far from certain.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Barry Callebaut will announce a restructuring action that includes either (a) a headcount reduction of at least 500 roles globally or (b) closure/sale of at least one production facility, with the announcement explicitly linked to demand weakness and/or cocoa price volatility.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Barry Callebaut releases a press statement/earnings release stating layoffs ≥500 or closure/sale of a facility and explicitly cites demand weakness and/or cocoa price volatility/collapse as a driver. NO if no such quantified restructuring is announced by deadline.
monitoring_signals: Barry Callebaut earnings releases and investor presentations
Union/work council notices and local plant news
Cocoa futures level/volatility and customer order commentary
Chocolate/food manufacturer demand indicators
predictions.rationale: A sharp reversal in cocoa pricing can create a volume and pricing reset; restructuring is plausible but management may stabilize via commercial actions instead of large, quantifiable cuts.
